tools.name: list_breakpoint_events
tools.description: List HELD (paused) breakpoint exchanges — the READ side of the breakpoint-continue flow (public.breakpoint_events; a row with resumed_at NULL is a request/response the proxy is holding RIGHT NOW, waiting for breakpoint_continue — feed it the returned request_id). YOUR OWN devices' events by default

tools.name: get_device
tools.description: Get one device row by uuid OR name — the DIRECT resolver for a KNOWN device: when you already hold a device uuid/name (from traffic rows, audit events, another tool's result), call this instead of browsing or paging list_devices. Returns the canonical record (uuid, name, model, os_version, setup, la

tools.name: list_devices

tools.name: get_my_account
tools.description: YOUR account at a glance — ONE self-scoped read returning the calling account's profile (name, display_name, email, role, sign-in providers, user_id, created_at), your per-user default connection type (own value + the global default + the effective fold), your linked Telegram accounts, the devices Y

tools.name: get_entry_count
tools.description: Count entries in a workspace (workspace_id or workspace_slug) with optional device/host/method/status/search filters.

tools.name: search_entries
tools.description: LOCATE captured entries by host/url substring (`q`). Optionally scope to a device_uuid and an absolute date range. Returns SLIM locator rows ({id, ts, device_uuid, kind, request_id, host, path, method, status, contentType, url} + a ~200-char requestBodyPreview/responseBodyPreview) — NOT full bodies;

tools.name: list_entries
tools.description: LIST the newest captured traffic entries as SLIM feed rows (id, ts, device, kind, request_id, host, path, method, status, contentType, url — NO bodies/headers) — the dashboard main live feed's MCP-parity read (#778). No search term needed (unlike search_entries' required `q`). Optionally scope to ON

tools.name: export_har
tools.description: Export captured entries as a HAR 1.2 log. Optionally scope by device, host, and date range.

tools.name: inspect_requests
tools.description: Deep-inspect FULL captured request/response detail for ONE host — method, URL, request + response HEADERS, and request + response BODIES (size-capped). tools.name: summarize_device_traffic

tools.name: list_service_groups
tools.description: List every service group (full rows incl. ssl_proxy_domains) with each group's primary_agent + ordered agents[] from the service_group_agents join.

tools.name: get_service_group
tools.description: Get one service group by id or name.

tools.name: list_tags
tools.description: List every tag (id, name, color, patterns).

tools.name: get_device_settings
tools.description: Read settings_device for one device (device_uuid or deviceName).

tools.name: get_subscription
tools.description: Return the caller's OWN Stripe subscription(s) — status/price/product/quantity/period/trial/cancel-at-period-end (from stripe_subscriptions, owner-scoped to the caller's OAuth sub). tools.name: list_invoices

tools.name: get_usage

tools.name: list_todos
tools.description: List YOUR to-dos from the /todo app (public.todos) — the shared "what to do / what's done" list you + the user both maintain. Owner-scoped to the calling account. tools.name: get_todo
tools.description: Get ONE of YOUR to-dos by id — its full row. Owner-scoped: an id you don't own returns not-found (never reveals another user's to-do). Returns { todo }.

tools.name: list_advisor_findings
tools.description: Browse the ADVISOR CENTER findings (public.advisor_findings) — the advisor-monitor's deduped audit/health findings you see on the dashboard /advisor view. tools.name: get_advisor_finding

Returns { found, finding, events:[{id,at,surface,action,

tools.description: List issue reports from the one-tap reporter (public.issue_reports — the durable rows behind report_issue + the dashboard ⌘⇧1 dialog). YOUR OWN reports by default (owner-scoped to the calling account — the same reach the RLS gives you), or EVERY reporter's with `all:true` (audit:view operators only;

tools.name: list_workspaces
tools.description: List workspaces (omit archived unless include_archived).

tools.name: get_workspace
tools.description: Get one workspace by id or slug.

tools.name: list_tabs
tools.description: List a workspace's tabs (workspace_id or workspace_slug).

tools.name: list_snapshots
tools.description: List snapshot files in the snapshots storage bucket.

tools.name: download_snapshot
tools.description: Get a 5-minute signed download URL for a snapshot file (name from list_snapshots).

tools.name: list_push_tokens
tools.description: List registered APNs push tokens (masked). Optionally scope to one device.

tools.name: get_push_response
tools.description: Read back a device's answer to an actionable push (#75) by the correlation_id returned from send_push. Returns the push_responses row { correlation_id, device_uuid, action (accept|reject|skip), context, responded_at, action_intent } or null if the user hasn't responded yet. action_intent (or null) c

tools.name: get_stats
tools.description: Return the live stats snapshot (get_stats RPC).

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Return the latest infra status snapshot (service_status) MERGED with the Edge tier's self-reported deployed build. findings.detail: Description of "list_devices" stacks urgency/override words (never, override). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "summarize_device_traffic" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: BOUNDED traffic aggregation for ONE device — the FIRST tool to call to report what a device is doing, find the busy/flooding host, or pick a host to clean up. R…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.busymate.dev" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_breakpoint_events, list_devices, get_my_account, search_entries, list_entries, inspect_requests, share_advisor_finding, list_issue_reports, download_snapshot, get_status · Private data: get_device, get_my_account, inspect_requests, list_snapshots, download_snapshot, list_push_tokens, get_status, list_audit_events, get_audit_event · Public sink: get_my_account, share_advisor_finding, list_audit_events · Destructive: share_advisor_finding, get_status.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_breakpoint_events, list_devices, get_my_account, search_entries, list_entries, inspect_requests, share_advisor_finding, list_issue_reports, download_snapshot, get_status · Private data: get_device, get_my_account, inspect_requests, list_snapshots, download_snapshot, list_push_tokens, get_status, list_audit_events, get_audit_event · Public sink: get_my_account, share_advisor_finding, list_audit_events · Destructive: share_advisor_finding, get_status

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

detail: Tool "list_devices" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "List devices — YOUR OWN by default (owner-scoped to the calling account: the same reach the dashboard gives you), or the WHOLE fleet with `all:true` (devices:view operators only; re-verified server-side — for a non-operator `all` is rejected, never silently widened). Each row is a slim projection: u" → after: "List devices — YOUR OWN by default (owner-scoped to the calling account: the same reach the dashboard gives you), or the WHOLE fleet with `all:true` (devices:view operators only; re-verified server-side — for a non-operator `all` is rejected, never silently widened). Each row is a slim projection: u"

remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

remediation.title: Tool description changed since last scan
remediation.detail: Tool "get_device_status" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Get the latest device_status row (online flag, last_heartbeat_at, ip, vpn_state, source)." → after: "Get a device's live status: `online` is DERIVED truth (#776 — devices.last_seen_at within 90s, 150s for farm hosts; the same derivation as every other surface, so it can't latch stale), plus the device_status row (last_heartbeat_at, ip, vpn_state, source, reported_online = the raw stored flag, diagn"

remediation.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
